tariffsPage.title: Annex No. 1
tariffsPage.subtitle: to the Public Offer for entering into an agreement for paid services to provide access to the modme.uz software under the SaaS model
tariffsPage.serviceDescription: for services providing access to the modme.uz Service
intro: This Annex defines the Tariffs, the procedure and terms of their application, and the specifics of settlements between MODME and the Customer.
The Annex is an integral part of the Public Offer and has equal legal force with its text.
Tariff information is also published on the Contractor's website in the Pricing section, where it may be updated and supplemented.
The cost of services is determined based on the Tariff, which depends on the number of students of the Customer.

For pricing purposes, a student means a unique individual learner for whom an electronic card or account is created in the Service and who has at least one active operation during the current billing period.
The number of students is determined on the last day of each billing period. The Customer manages the student list in its Account and is responsible for keeping it up to date.

If the actual number of students exceeds the maximum limit of the paid Tariff, the Customer is automatically moved to the next applicable Tariff. The difference must be paid within 5 working days after receiving the invoice.
The Customer may upgrade to a higher Tariff at any time by making the relevant additional payment. Downgrading to a Tariff with fewer students is possible only after the paid access period ends.
If this Annex differs from information on the website, the version published on the website prevails.
notes: All Tariffs are set in the national currency, Uzbek soums (UZS), and are stated without VAT.
A Tariff payment is the amount paid by the Customer for the Contractor's services providing access to the Service under the selected Tariff.
The Contractor may change Tariffs, prices and terms of application by publishing a new version on the website.
Changes take effect from the date of publication unless otherwise stated in the text of the changes.
For already paid periods, the price and terms of the Tariff remain valid until the end of the paid period.
The Customer may purchase additional services not included in the Tariff under a separate agreement, such as staff training, CRM setup or integrations.
Such services are paid under a separate agreement.
tariffsPage.contactIntro: Questions related to Tariffs and payment may be sent:
tariffsPage.telegramPrefix: or via the official Telegram manager account, the link to which is published on the Service website:

technicalSupportPage.title: Annex No. 2
technicalSupportPage.subtitle: to the Public Offer for entering into an agreement for paid services to provide access to the modme.uz software under the SaaS model
paragraphs: This Annex defines the procedure, terms and response timeframes for technical support provided by MODME to Customers of the modme.uz Service.
This Policy is an annex to the Public Offer and the Agreement and is published openly at https://modme.uz.
Technical support is provided to Customers within the paid tariff plan and is an integral part of the Services.

Technical support includes consulting and operational support for the Service, help with functionality, registration and processing of incidents and requests, collection of feedback, notifications about updates and planned maintenance, API and integration questions, and forwarding feature requests to the development team.

Technical support does not include Customer staff training outside the Service functionality, individual business-process customization not covered by documentation, or correction of errors caused by Customer actions.
Support requests are accepted through official communication channels.
Technical support working hours are Monday through Saturday, 10:00 to 19:00 Tashkent time (UTC+5), excluding official public holidays of the Republic of Uzbekistan.
All Customer requests are registered in the MODME CRM system.

The Contractor undertakes to provide an initial response within 1 working day after receiving a request. An initial response means confirmation of receipt, a request for additional information or the start of diagnostics.

As part of the initial response, the support specialist diagnoses the issue and takes steps to resolve it, including consultations, instructions, photo or video materials, documentation links or an online consultation.
If resolution requires a product manager or the development team, the request is forwarded to the relevant department and the Customer is informed about further timeframes.
After an incident is resolved or a request is completed, support contacts the Customer again to confirm the solution and collect feedback.
MODME support also performs proactive work: checking platform operation, collecting service quality feedback and gathering suggestions for Service improvement.
Requests to MODME technical support are accepted through the following official channels:

cybersecurityPage.title: Appendix No. 4
cybersecurityPage.subtitle: to the Public Offer for the Conclusion of a Contract for the Provision of Paid Services for Access to the “modme.uz” Software under the SaaS Model

blocks.text: 1.1. This Policy defines the basic principles and measures for ensuring cybersecurity when users interact with the “modme.uz” Service (hereinafter referred to as the Service) and applies to all clients, employees, and partners of MODME LLC (TIN 307442900) (hereinafter referred to as “MODME”).

1.2. The purpose of this document is to ensure the integrity, availability, and confidentiality of data, as well as to prevent incidents related to the loss, alteration, or unauthorized disclosure of information processed by the Service.
1.3. This Policy is an appendix to the Public Offer and the Agreement and is publicly available on the website https://modme.uz.
1.4. This Policy has been drafted in accordance with:

items: The Law of the Republic of Uzbekistan “On Cybersecurity”;
The Law of the Republic of Uzbekistan “On Personal Data”;
other regulatory acts and internal regulations of MODME.

blocks.text: In ensuring data security, MODME is guided by the following principles:

items: Continuity of protection: security is built into all stages of the Service’s lifecycle—from design to maintenance.
Confidentiality: access to data is granted only to authorized persons.
Integrity: prevention of unauthorized changes to data and program code.
Availability: ensuring uninterrupted operation of the Service with minimal downtime.
Transparency: users are informed about security measures and incidents that may affect their data.
Accountability: every person with access to MODME systems is personally responsible for complying with security rules.

blocks.text: 3.1. Technical Measures:

items: use of secure communication protocols (HTTPS, SSL/TLS);
data backup at least once a day;
storing data on servers located within the territory of the Republic of Uzbekistan;
using firewalls, antivirus, and anti-DDoS protection;
regularly updating software and operating systems.

blocks.text: 3.2. Organizational Measures:

items: restricting access to administrative panels via IP filtering;
mandatory two-factor authentication for employees and partners;
password rotation and access key management;
maintaining event logs and storing them for at least 12 months;
annual security testing and audits conducted by independent specialists.

blocks.text: 4.1. Upon detecting a security incident or signs thereof (data breach, hacking, virus attack, equipment failure), MODME shall immediately:

items: record the event in the incident log;
block the sources of the threat;
notify affected customers (if their data is affected);
initiate an internal investigation and address the consequences.

blocks.text: 4.2. The cybersecurity officer prepares an incident report and takes measures to prevent similar incidents.

4.3. In the event of incidents involving personal data or threatening the information infrastructure, MODME notifies the State Cybersecurity Center and other competent authorities in accordance with the procedure established by the legislation of the Republic of Uzbekistan.
5.1. Users are required to:

items: keep their usernames and passwords confidential;
not share access with third parties;
use modern and secure devices and browsers;
immediately notify MODME support (crmmodme@gmail.com) upon detecting suspicious activity.

blocks.text: 5.2. The user is responsible for actions performed under their account until MODME is notified of a security breach.
6.1. MODME engages only certified providers of hosting, telecommunications, SMS notification, and payment system services that ensure an adequate level of cybersecurity.
6.2. Data transfer to third parties is carried out exclusively under contract and in accordance with internal approval and security audit procedures.
7.1. All MODME employees receive cybersecurity training upon hiring and undergo annual refresher training.
7.2. Employees sign a non-disclosure agreement regarding confidential information and personal data.
7.3. MODME conducts internal incident response tests and drills at least once a year.
8.1. The person designated by order is responsible for organizing and overseeing cybersecurity measures.
8.2. MODME conducts an internal cybersecurity audit and submits a report to the company director at least once a year.

8.3. Employees and contractors are subject to disciplinary and other liability for violations of this Policy in accordance with the laws of the Republic of Uzbekistan and the company’s internal regulations.
9.1. This Policy is publicly available on the website https://modme.uz in the “Documents” section.

privacyProcessingPage.title: Appendix No. 3
privacyProcessingPage.subtitle: to the Public Offer for the Conclusion of a Contract for the Provision of Paid Services for Access to the “modme.uz” Software under the SaaS Model
privacyProcessingPage.documentSubtitle: on the Processing of Personal Data
intro: This Personal Data Processing Policy (hereinafter referred to as the “Policy”) has been developed in accordance with the legislation of the Republic of Uzbekistan in the field of personal data protection, including the Law of the Republic of Uzbekistan “On Personal Data,” the Law “On Informatization,” as well as other regulatory legal acts of the Republic of Uzbekistan.

The Policy defines the procedures and principles for the processing of personal data carried out by MODME Limited Liability Company (hereinafter referred to as MODME, the Operator) in the course of its activities.

This Policy applies to all personal data that MODME receives and processes when interacting: with users of MODME’s electronic platforms (including the website, mobile app, API interfaces, messengers, and other digital resources); with MODME’s clients and partners in the context of providing services, concluding, and executing contracts.

MODME LLC provides services to educational organizations (schools, training centers, and other institutions), processing: the personal data of its own users and partners — as the Controller and Data Owner; the personal data of clients of educational organizations (students, parents, employees) — as a Third Party, solely for the purpose of storing and ensuring the functioning of the CRM system.

MODME LLC is not responsible for the accuracy, completeness, or timeliness of data entered by users or representatives of educational organizations, and does not use such data for any purpose other than storage, technical maintenance, and ensuring the secure operation of the service.

Before using MODME LLC’s electronic platforms, users are advised to review this Policy. By providing personal data to MODME, the user confirms their consent to its processing in accordance with the terms of this Policy.

sections.title: 1. Terms and Definitions
paragraphs: For the purposes of this Policy, the following key terms are used:
Personal data database — a collection of organized personal data stored in an information system that enables the storage, modification, retrieval, and use of data subjects’ personal data.

Personal data — any information about a data subject recorded in electronic, written, or other form that allows for the direct or indirect identification of that person when using MODME’s services or electronic platforms.

Data Subject (hereinafter referred to as the “Subject”) — a natural person (user, client, customer, or representative of an organization) who uses, has used, or intends to use the services provided by MODME, or otherwise interacts with MODME.

The owner of the personal data database (hereinafter referred to as the “Owner”) is a legal entity that has the right to own, use, and dispose of the personal data database. Under this Policy, such an entity is MODME LLC (TIN 307442900).

The personal data controller (hereinafter referred to as the “Controller”) is a legal entity that, independently or jointly with other entities, organizes and/or carries out the processing of personal data, as well as determines the purposes, scope, and actions performed with personal data. In this Policy, the Controller is MODME LLC.

A third party is any natural or legal person who is not a Data Subject, Data Owner, or Data Controller, but who participates in the processing of personal data by virtue of an instruction, contract, or other legal basis (for example, educational organizations that are MODME clients).

The MODME electronic platform (hereinafter referred to as the “Electronic Platform”) is the collection of MODME’s digital resources, including the website, CRM system, API interfaces, as well as official pages and bots in messaging apps, through which services are provided and interactions with Data Subjects take place.

Processing of personal data — any action (operation) or set of actions (operations) performed on personal data, including collection, systematization, storage, modification, supplementation, use, provision, transfer (including cross-border transfer), anonymization, and destruction of data — with or without the use of automation tools.

Confidentiality of personal data — a mandatory requirement established for the Owner, the Operator, and Third Parties to prevent the dissemination or provision of personal data to third parties without the Subject’s consent or in the absence of another legal basis.
Services — services for providing access to the MODME software, ensuring its operation, and providing technical support to users, rendered by the Operator through the MODME Electronic Platforms.

sections.title: 2. Personal data Owner and Operator and contact information
paragraphs: 2.1. MODME Limited Liability Company (TIN 307442900), registered at the following address: Republic of Uzbekistan, Tashkent, Mirzo-Ulugbek District, 4a Tepamasjid Street, Navnikhol MFY, is the owner and operator of personal data stored and processed during the use of the MODME Electronic Platforms.
2.2. MODME acts as:
the controller of personal data with respect to information obtained during the registration and use of the Service by the Customer and its representatives;
a third party with respect to personal data posted by the Customer on the Service (including data regarding clients, pupils, students, parents, and employees).

2.3. MODME processes the personal data of its Customers’ data subjects exclusively at the Customer’s request and to the extent necessary to fulfill contractual obligations and ensure the functioning of the software.
2.4. Contact information for questions related to the processing of personal data:
e-mail: crmmodme@gmail.com

sections.title: 3. Application of the Policy
paragraphs: 3.1. Before using MODME’s Electronic Platforms, the Data Subject is advised to review this Personal Data Processing Policy.

3.2. Use of any MODME Electronic Platforms, including the website, CRM system, and Telegram bots, constitutes the Data Subject’s unconditional consent to this Policy and the terms and conditions for the processing of their personal data.
Such consent covers:
the processing of personal data by the Operator and Third Parties for the purpose of providing services to grant access to MODME software, as well as its technical support and maintenance;
notifying the Data Subject about new services, promotions, and features of the Service;
conducting surveys, questionnaires, marketing, and analytical research, including communication via the means of communication specified on the Operator’s Electronic Platforms.

3.3. This consent includes authorization to perform all necessary actions regarding personal data, including the collection, organization, accumulation, storage, clarification (updating, modification), use, transfer, anonymization, blocking, and destruction of data provided by the Data Subject when using the Electronic Platforms or during written or verbal communication with MODME.

3.4. When processing personal data, the Operator is guided by the requirements of the Law of the Republic of Uzbekistan “On Personal Data,” the Law “On Informatization,” as well as other regulatory legal acts of the Republic of Uzbekistan.
3.5. The MODME website does not collect or process personal data.

Clicking the “Get Demo Version,” “Sales Department,” and other similar elements redirects the user to MODME’s official resources on Telegram, where further interaction takes place. By providing their data to the Telegram bot or when communicating with a MODME manager, the user confirms that they have read this Policy and consent to the processing of their personal data in accordance with its terms.
3.6. By performing such implied actions, the Data Subject confirms that they have read this Policy and consent to the processing of their personal data.
3.7. Entering into a contract with MODME, including through acceptance of a public offer, also constitutes a basis for the processing of the Data Subject’s personal data.

3.8. If the Data Subject does not agree to the terms of personal data processing, they are not entitled to use the Operator’s services and must not provide their personal data through MODME’s Electronic Platforms.

sections.title: 4. Categories of processed personal data
paragraphs: 4.1. When using MODME’s Electronic Platforms, the following categories of personal data may be processed:
data provided by the Customer during registration (company name, TIN, contact person, phone number, email address, and other information necessary for the conclusion and performance of the contract);
data that the Customer or its employees post on the Service when working with clients, students, or employees of educational institutions;
technical data automatically collected when using the Service (IP address, browser type, operating system version information, log files, cookies, etc.).

4.2. The Operator does not collect or process the Customer’s clients’ personal data on its own. Such data is processed exclusively at the Customer’s request and under the Customer’s responsibility to the extent necessary for the performance of contractual obligations.

4.3. The Operator does not verify the accuracy of the personal data provided by the Data Subject and does not assess the Data Subject’s legal capacity. All personal data provided by the Data Subject is presumed to be accurate until evidence to the contrary is received.

4.4. The Operator and Third Parties are entitled to use the aforementioned data to test, improve, and develop technological solutions, optimize services, analyze risks, and conduct statistical and financial research, based on the Operator’s legitimate interests and within the framework of the legislation of the Republic of Uzbekistan.
4.5. Personal data is used only for the purposes for which it was collected, provided that the necessary level of personal data protection is maintained.
4.6. Access to personal data is granted only to employees of the Operator and/or Third Parties whose job duties are directly related to its processing.

Such employees are obligated to maintain confidentiality and prevent the disclosure of personal data that has come to their knowledge in the course of performing their employment or contractual duties.

sections.title: 5. Conditions and Principles for the Processing of Personal Data
paragraphs: 5.1. The processing of personal data at MODME is carried out in accordance with the principles established by the Law of the Republic of Uzbekistan “On Personal Data,” namely:
lawfulness and fairness;
limiting processing to the achievement of specific, predetermined, and lawful purposes;
prohibition on combining databases containing personal data processed for incompatible purposes;
accuracy, adequacy, and relevance of personal data;
retaining personal data no longer than is necessary for the purposes of their processing.
5.2. The grounds for processing personal data are:
obtaining the Data Subject’s consent to data processing;
the necessity of processing to fulfill a contract (including one concluded through acceptance of a public offer) or to take actions at the Data Subject’s request prior to concluding a contract;
fulfillment of the Operator’s legal obligations;
protection of the vital interests of the Data Subject or another person;
pursuit of the legitimate interests of the Operator or a Third Party, provided that this does not infringe upon the rights and freedoms of the Data Subject;
use of data for statistical or research purposes, provided that the data is anonymized;
processing of data obtained from publicly available sources.
5.3. The scope of personal data requested and processed by MODME is limited by the principle of data minimization and strictly corresponds to the stated purposes of processing.
5.4. The purposes of processing personal data include:
identifying the Data Subject registered on the Electronic Platform for the conclusion and performance of the contract;
providing access to personalized resources of the Electronic Platform;
establishing communication, sending notifications, requests, and responses related to the use of the Service and the provision of services;
verifying the accuracy of the data provided;
creating and administering an account;
processing and receiving payments;
providing customer and technical support;
conducting statistical, marketing, and other analytical studies based on anonymized data.
5.5. Personal data is protected by organizational and technical security measures designed to prevent unauthorized access, alteration, distribution, destruction, or other unlawful use of the data.
5.6. If necessary to protect the rights and legitimate interests of the Data Subject, personal data may be processed without prior consent until such time as obtaining consent becomes possible.

5.7. This Policy applies to the processing of personal data regardless of the form and method of its provision (on the website, in the app, by phone, via messengers, or otherwise) and regardless of the form of storage (in information systems or on paper).
5.8. For a minor Data Subject, consent to the processing of personal data is given by their parents (guardians, custodians), and in their absence—by the guardianship and custody authorities.
5.9. In the event of the Data Subject’s death, consent to the processing of their personal data is given by their heirs, unless such consent was previously provided by the Data Subject themselves.
5.10. The processing of personal data of a Data Subject declared deceased by a court or recognized as missing is permitted on the basis of the written consent of their heirs.

sections.title: 6. Cookies and Technical Data
paragraphs: 6.1. In connection with the operation of the MODME Electronic Platforms, the Operator may collect certain information using cookie technology and may also obtain information regarding users’ IP addresses. Such data is processed in accordance with this Policy and the separate Cookie Policy posted on the MODME website.

6.2. Cookies allow information about a user’s actions and settings to be stored on their device to ensure the proper functioning of the Electronic Platforms and to improve the quality of service. MODME uses both session and persistent cookies.
Session cookies are deleted when the browser is closed;
Persistent cookies remain on the device until they expire or are deleted by the user.

6.3. MODME may allow certain third parties (including providers of advertising, analytical, or technical services) to collect information using cookies and other technologies directly on MODME’s web pages.
Such third parties process information in accordance with their own privacy policies, and the Operator is not liable for their actions unless they violate the laws of the Republic of Uzbekistan.

6.4. Technical information (e.g., IP addresses, browser information, access time, URL of the requested page) is used to ensure system functionality, analyze errors, maintain network security, and aggregate anonymized statistics (e.g., by region or device type).

sections.title: 7. Retention Period for Personal Data
paragraphs: 7.1. The Controller retains the personal data of Data Subjects for the period necessary to achieve the purposes of processing and until all grounds for retention cease to exist, including:
the term of the contract between the Data Subject and the Controller;
the existence of the Controller’s legitimate interests in the provision of services;
the existence of data retention obligations established by law;
the absence of a request from the Data Subject to cease processing and delete the data.
7.2. Upon the cessation of the aforementioned circumstances, personal data shall be deleted or anonymized in accordance with established procedures.

7.3. The Operator shall cease processing and destroy personal data within 10 (ten) calendar days of receiving a written request from the Data Subject or upon expiration of the retention period, unless otherwise provided by law.

7.4. The deletion of anonymized and backup copies of personal data stored in the Operator’s archives and technical systems may take up to 60 (sixty) calendar days, due to the technical characteristics of information storage.

sections.title: 8. Cross-border Transfer of Personal Data
paragraphs: 8.1. A cross-border transfer of personal data is the transfer of personal data by the Controller and/or the Data Subject outside the territory of the Republic of Uzbekistan.

8.2. Cross-border transfer of personal data is permitted to the territory of foreign states that ensure an adequate level of protection of the rights of data subjects, as established in accordance with the procedure provided for by the legislation of the Republic of Uzbekistan.
8.3. The transfer of personal data to the territory of foreign states that do not ensure an adequate level of protection may be carried out in the following cases:
if the Data Subject has given written (including electronic) consent to the cross-border transfer of their personal data;
when necessary to protect the constitutional order, public order, the rights and freedoms of citizens, and the health and morality of the population;
in cases provided for by international treaties of the Republic of Uzbekistan;
if the cross-border transfer is necessary for the performance of a contract to which the Data Subject is a party.

8.4. Cross-border transfer of personal data may be restricted or prohibited for the purpose of protecting the foundations of the constitutional order, morality, health, rights, and legitimate interests of citizens of the Republic of Uzbekistan, and ensuring national defense and state security.

8.5. When transferring personal data via the Telegram messenger, including the use of official MODME bots and communication with MODME managers, the Data Subject independently and on their own initiative provides their data to the specified service.

8.6. The Data Subject is responsible for the accuracy and scope of the personal data transmitted via Telegram and undertakes to independently review Telegram’s Privacy Policy, which governs the processing and storage of user data on servers located outside the territory of the Republic of Uzbekistan.
8.7. The transfer of personal data to Telegram is classified as a cross-border transfer of personal data carried out at the initiative of the Data Subject.

8.8. MODME is not responsible for the processing, storage, distribution, or other use of the Data Subject’s personal data by the Telegram messenger, which operates in accordance with its own rules and privacy policy.

8.9. After the transfer of personal data to Telegram, its subsequent processing by MODME LLC is carried out exclusively for the purposes set forth in this Policy and within the scope of the granted authority.

sections.title: 9. Rights of the Data Subject
paragraphs: 9.1. The data subject has the right to:
know whether the Data Controller, Data Processor, or a third party holds their personal data, as well as the nature and source of such data;
receive, upon request, information regarding the processing of personal data, including the purposes, legal grounds, retention periods, and a list of persons to whom the data has been disclosed;
receive information regarding the conditions for accessing their personal data;

request the modification, supplementation, temporary suspension, or destruction of their personal data if it is incomplete, outdated, inaccurate, obtained unlawfully, or not required for the stated purposes of processing;
give consent to the processing of their personal data and withdraw such consent, except in cases where processing is carried out pursuant to law;
consent to the Owner, Operator, and/or a Third Party to disseminate their data in publicly available sources;
address issues regarding the protection of their rights and legitimate interests to the authorized state body for the protection of personal data or to a court.
9.2. On behalf of a Data Subject who has been declared legally incompetent or has limited legal capacity, the legal representative shall manage their personal data.

9.3. The Data Subject is obligated to provide accurate personal data in cases where such provision is necessary for compliance with legislation, the protection of citizens’ rights and legitimate interests, and the assurance of national defense and state security.

9.4. The Data Subject has the right to submit a request to the Controller regarding access to their personal data, its supplementation, correction, deletion, or restriction of processing in one of the following ways:
by sending an email stating the purpose of the request and attaching a copy of an identity document to the email address: crmmodme@gmail.com;
by sending a written request to the postal address: Republic of Uzbekistan, Tashkent, Mirzo-Ulugbek District, Tepamasjid Street, Building 4a.

9.5. Upon receipt of the Data Subject’s request, the Controller verifies the applicant’s identity, reviews the request, and provides a response within 10 (ten) calendar days from the date of receipt, unless other timeframes are established by law. The response is sent via the same method used to submit the request.

9.6. The Data Subject has the right to withdraw previously given consent to the processing of personal data at any time. Withdrawal is carried out in accordance with the procedure set forth in this Policy and does not affect the lawfulness of processing carried out prior to the withdrawal.

sections.title: 10. Rights and Obligations of the Owner and/or Operator
paragraphs: 10.1. The Owner and/or Operator have the right to process personal data within the limits established by the legislation of the Republic of Uzbekistan and this Policy.
10.2. The Owner and/or Operator are obligated to:
comply with the requirements of personal data legislation;
provide the Data Subject with information regarding the processing of their personal data upon request;
approve the list of personal data necessary and sufficient to achieve the purposes of processing;
take measures to destroy personal data upon achieving the purpose of processing or at the Subject’s request;
provide evidence of obtaining the Subject’s consent to the processing of their personal data, if required by law;
make changes or additions to personal data upon documentary confirmation of the accuracy of the new information, or destroy the data if such changes cannot be made;
temporarily suspend the processing or destroy personal data upon detection of violations of the processing conditions;
ensure that the Data Subject can submit an electronic request to suspend or delete personal data;
notify the Data Subject and other parties involved in the processing of any changes, destruction, or restriction of access to the data;
notify the Data Subject in the event of the transfer of their personal data to a third party;
register all personal data databases owned and/or processed;
take legal, organizational, and technical measures to protect personal data from unauthorized access and other threats.
10.3. The Owner and (or) the Operator has the right to entrust the processing of personal data to a Third Party in the following cases:
the Subject’s consent in written or electronic form;
the necessity to fulfill a contract between the Owner and the Subject or to perform actions at the Subject’s request prior to the conclusion of a contract;
cases expressly provided for by the legislation of the Republic of Uzbekistan.

10.4. The obligations of the Owner, the Operator, and third parties regarding the protection of personal data arise from the moment of receipt of such data and remain in effect until the data is destroyed or anonymized.

10.5. The Owner and/or the Operator shall appoint an official responsible for the processing and protection of personal data, who shall monitor compliance with the requirements of the law and the provisions of this Policy.

sections.title: 11. Procedure for Providing Information Regarding the Processing of Personal Data
paragraphs: 11.1. The data subject has the right to modify, supplement, or update the personal data they have provided using the editing functions on the MODME Electronic Platforms or by submitting a written request to the Operator.

11.2. The Data Subject has the right to request the deletion of their personal data by contacting the Operator via the MODME website, the Telegram bot, or by sending a written or electronic request to the Operator’s address.
11.3. The destruction of personal data refers to actions that render it impossible to recover the personal data by any means.
11.4. Personal data shall be destroyed by the Owner, the Operator, or a Third Party in the following cases:
achievement of the purposes of personal data processing;
withdrawal by the Data Subject of consent to their processing;
expiration of the processing period specified in the Data Subject’s consent;
entry into force of a court decision or a requirement from an authorized state body.
11.5. The Data Subject may withdraw consent to the processing of personal data in the same form in which such consent was given, or in writing, including in the form of an electronic document.
11.6. The Data Subject has the right to receive information regarding the processing of their personal data, including:
confirmation of the fact that personal data is being processed;
the legal grounds and purposes of the processing;
the methods of processing used;
the name and address of the Data Controller (Operator), and information about persons who have access to personal data or receive it on the basis of a contract or by law;
the composition of the personal data and the source from which it was obtained, unless otherwise provided by law;
the duration of the processing and storage of personal data;
information regarding any actual or intended cross-border transfer of personal data.

11.7. The Data Subject’s right to receive information may be restricted if its disclosure violates the rights and legitimate interests of other persons or impedes the administration of justice, investigative, or operational-search activities.
11.8. The procedure for providing information about personal data to third parties is determined by the terms of the Data Subject’s consent to the processing of personal data.
11.9. The Data Controller and/or Data Processor are exempt from the obligation to provide information to the Data Subject in cases where:
the Data Subject has previously been notified of the processing of their personal data;
the personal data has been made publicly available by the Data Subject or obtained from open sources;
the provision of information would lead to a violation of the rights and legitimate interests of third parties.
11.10. A notice of refusal to provide information shall be sent to the Data Subject in writing or electronically within 10 (ten) calendar days of receiving the request.
11.11. The Data Subject has the right to appeal the decision to refuse to provide information to the authorized state body for the protection of personal data or to a court.

sections.title: 12. Notification of Actions Involving the Processing of Personal Data
paragraphs: 12.1. When the Data Subject’s personal data is entered into the database, the Operator shall notify the Data Subject of the purposes, legal grounds, and anticipated scope of such processing.

12.2. In the event of a transfer of personal data to a third party, the Owner and/or the Operator shall notify the Data Subject within three business days of such transfer, unless otherwise provided by law or by an agreement with the Data Subject.
12.3. The Data Subject shall not be notified in the following cases:
processing of personal data by government agencies within the scope of their authority;
transfer of data for historical, statistical, sociological, or scientific purposes, provided that the data is anonymized;
collection of personal data from publicly available sources.

sections.title: 13. Automated and Non-Automated Processing of Personal Data
paragraphs: 13.1. Personal data is processed both using automated means and without them (in a mixed form), including actions related to the collection, systematization, storage, clarification (updating, modification), use, transfer, and destruction of personal data—for the purposes established by this Policy.

13.2. The data subject has the right not to be subject to decisions based solely on the automated processing of their personal data if such decisions affect their rights, legitimate interests, or entail legal consequences, except in cases provided for by the legislation of the Republic of Uzbekistan and this Policy.
13.3. Decisions based solely on the automated processing of personal data may be made in the following cases:
with the Subject’s written consent, including an electronic document signed in accordance with established procedures;
if such a decision is made in fulfillment of a contract between the Owner (and/or the Operator) and the Data Subject, or as part of the fulfillment of the terms of a previously concluded contract;
in cases expressly provided for by the legislation of the Republic of Uzbekistan.

13.4. The Owner and/or Operator shall explain to the Data Subject the procedure and principles for making decisions based exclusively on automated processing, the possible legal consequences of such decisions, and shall also provide the Data Subject with the opportunity to file an objection and exercise the right to protect their legitimate interests.

13.5. Upon receipt of an objection from the Data Subject, the Data Controller and/or Data Processor shall review it and notify the Data Subject in writing or electronically of the results of the review no later than 10 (ten) calendar days from the date of receipt of the request.

sections.title: 14. Special Conditions for the Processing of Personal Data of Citizens of the Republic of Uzbekistan
paragraphs: 14.1. When processing the personal data of citizens of the Republic of Uzbekistan using information technology, including processing on the World Wide Web, the Data Controller and/or Data Processor shall store such data in personal data databases located on technical equipment physically situated within the territory of the Republic of Uzbekistan, and registered in the State Register of Personal Data Bases in accordance with the procedure established by the authorized state body.

14.2. The storage and processing of personal data of citizens of the Republic of Uzbekistan outside the territory of the Republic of Uzbekistan is permitted only if the requirements of the law are met, adequate data protection is ensured, and there is consent from the Data Subject or a contract to which the Data Subject is a party.

sections.title: 15. Guarantees for the Protection of Personal Data
paragraphs: 15.1. The State guarantees the protection of personal data in accordance with the legislation of the Republic of Uzbekistan.
15.2. The Data Controller, the Data Processor, and third parties involved in the processing of personal data shall take legal, organizational, and technical measures aimed at ensuring:
the exercise of the Data Subject’s right to protection against interference in their private and family life;
the integrity, security, and accuracy of personal data;
compliance with the confidentiality regime for personal data;
the prevention of unauthorized or unlawful access, alteration, dissemination, and destruction of personal data.

sections.title: 16. Confidentiality of Personal Data
paragraphs: 16.1. The confidentiality of personal data refers to the requirement—binding on the Data Controller, the Data Processor, and any other persons who have access to personal data—that such data may not be disclosed or disseminated without the Data Subject’s consent or another lawful basis.

16.2. The Owner, the Operator, and other persons who have access to personal data are obligated not to disclose or disseminate such data without the Subject’s consent, except in cases expressly provided for by the legislation of the Republic of Uzbekistan.

sections.title: 17. Publicly Available Personal Data
paragraphs: 17.1. Personal data is considered publicly available if access to it is unrestricted with the Data Subject’s consent, or if it is not subject to confidentiality requirements.

17.2. For the purpose of providing information to the public, publicly available sources of personal data may be created, including biographical directories, telephone and address books, as well as open-access electronic information resources.

17.3. With the Data Subject’s written consent, publicly accessible sources of personal data may include the Data Subject’s last name, first name, patronymic, year and place of birth, address, subscriber number, information about their profession, and other data provided by the Data Subject.

17.4. Information about the Data Subject shall be removed from publicly available sources of personal data upon the Data Subject’s request, submitted in the form in which consent was given or in writing (including an electronic document), as well as by decision of an authorized state body or a court.

sections.title: 18. Final Provisions
paragraphs: 18.1. All disputes and disagreements arising from the processing of personal data shall be resolved in accordance with the procedures established by the laws of the Republic of Uzbekistan.
18.2. Persons found guilty of violating personal data laws shall be held liable in accordance with the applicable laws of the Republic of Uzbekistan.

sections.title: 19. Amendments to the Policy
paragraphs: 19.1. MODME LLC reserves the right to amend this Policy without prior notice to Data Subjects.
19.2. The new version of the Policy shall take effect upon its publication on MODME’s Electronic Platforms, unless otherwise specified in the document itself.
19.3. Changes in legislation regarding personal data protection, as well as decisions by authorized government agencies, constitute grounds for amending this Policy.
19.4. This Policy is approved by order of the director of MODME LLC.

metadata.title: Consent to personal data processing - Modme
metadata.description: Consent to personal data processing and link to the Modme CRM personal data processing policy

consent.title: CONSENT TO PERSONAL DATA PROCESSING
consent.intro: This consent is provided by an individual who represents a legal entity or individual entrepreneur entering into an agreement with MODME LLC.

items.text: I, the personal data subject, confirm that I have read the Personal Data Processing Policy of MODME LLC (MODME), published on the official website https://modme.uz, and give unconditional and explicit consent to MODME LLC (TIN 307442900) to process my personal data in accordance with the laws of the Republic of Uzbekistan and the Contractor's Policy.
My personal data is processed for the following purposes:
list: registration and provision of access to the modme.uz Service;
performance of contractual obligations between me (or the organization I represent) and MODME;
operation of the CRM/LMS platform and related services;
sending notifications, technical information and messages related to use of the Service;
compliance with the laws of the Republic of Uzbekistan.

items.text: The following personal data may be processed under this consent:
list: information entered during registration or use of the Service functionality.

items.text: MODME may collect, record, systematize, store, change, use, anonymize, block, delete and destroy personal data, and transfer it within the Republic of Uzbekistan for the purposes stated in this Consent.
Data is processed using automated tools and, where necessary, manually in accordance with the MODME Personal Data Processing Policy and legal requirements.
Data is stored on protected servers located in the Republic of Uzbekistan in accordance with the database registration certificate.
MODME may transfer personal data to third parties where such transfer is necessary for:
list: hosting and data-center services in the Republic of Uzbekistan;
other purposes provided by the laws of the Republic of Uzbekistan.

items.text: I have been informed that I may receive information about the composition and processing methods of my data, request correction, blocking or deletion of data, withdraw consent by sending a notice to crmmodme@gmail.com, and appeal MODME actions as provided by law.
This consent is valid throughout my use of the Service and for sixty (60) calendar days after relations with MODME end.
After this period, data must be deleted or anonymized under the Offer and MODME Personal Data Processing Policy unless otherwise required by law.
I confirm that the provided data is accurate and consent to its processing under this document.

consent.fullDocTitle: Annex No. 3 — Privacy and Personal Data Processing Policy
